title: Abortion Providers Forced to Adapt After Court Ruling Blocks Pill Access by Mail
summary: The Fifth Circuit court’s ruling, which is being appealed, reinstates a requirement that patients visit a health care provider in person to obtain mifepristone, upending abortion access in the United States.

title: Supreme Court Asked to Restore Access to Abortion Pill by Mail
summary: A federal appeals court temporarily halted a Food and Drug Administration regulation that has greatly expanded access to the abortion pill mifepristone.

title: Trump Says He Is Reviewing Iran’s Latest Offer but Doubts It Is Acceptable
summary: The comments came one day after Mr. Trump flatly rejected the proposal. He later clarified he was only briefed on the “concept of the deal.”

title: U.S. Fast-Tracks Arms Deals Valued at $8.6 Billion to Mideast Partners
summary: The Persian Gulf countries and Israel have faced repeated Iranian attacks during the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran. The State Department move bypassed congressional review.

title: What to Know About the U.S.-Iran Peace Talks
summary: Negotiations to end the war are at an impasse over Iran’s nuclear program and the Strait of Hormuz, which remains mostly shut.

title: U.S. to Withdraw 5,000 Troops From Germany. But Thousands Will Remain.
summary: The U.S. military has tens of thousands of personnel in Germany, more than in any other foreign country but Japan. Their significance extends well beyond Europe.

title: Trump’s Vision for D.C. ‘Garden of Heroes’ Statues Grows in Size and Cost
summary: Plans for the project include formal gardens, reflecting pools and plazas alongside the statues of 250 notable Americans, according to documents obtained by The New York Times.

title: The Americans Who Could Be in Trump’s ‘Garden of Heroes’ Statue Park in D.C.
summary: President Trump plans to build a park along the Potomac River featuring life-size statues of 250 Americans.

title: The Cannabis Industry’s New Best Friend? President Trump
summary: The administration’s decision to relax federal regulations on medical marijuana comes with big tax breaks for many cannabis companies, and could drive new investment in the budding sector.

title: The Head-Turning Hats of the 2026 Kentucky Derby
summary: At the 152nd annual horse race at Churchill Downs, attendees trotted out some eye-catching ensembles.

title: Delayed Louisiana Primaries Stoke Confusion at Ballot Box
summary: On the first day of early voting, some glimpses of voter bewilderment came into focus, part of a dizzying scramble playing out days after a major Supreme Court ruling.

title: An Insurgency Threatens U.S. Mining Ambitions in Pakistan
summary: Attacks by the Baloch Liberation Army could derail Pakistan’s plans for a billion-dollar mining deal with the Trump administration.

title: A Perilous Train Ride Home in Pakistan on the Jaffer Express
summary: We witnessed crossfire while waiting for the Jaffer Express, a vital lifeline through Balochistan. For many, it’s the only way home.

title: The Race Is On to Find the Treasure Buried in San Francisco
summary: From the Gold Rush to the A.I. boom, San Francisco has always drawn those seeking riches. Now, people are deciphering riddles and digging up the city to find a box filled with cold, hard cash.

title: With Iran, the U.S. Stands to Lose Its Reputation, Its Friends or Its Soul
summary: President Trump has overextended the empire dangerously.

title: Trump Shouldn’t Expect Much From His China Trip
summary: China no longer looks to an erratic America as a model to emulate, but as a distraction to be managed.

title: Spirit Airlines Cancels All Flights, Stranding Passengers
summary: The budget carrier abruptly canceled flights early on Saturday, leaving passengers to rush to make other plans. “Even if they go back into business, never again,” one traveler said.

title: South Korea’s Cherry Blossoms Attract a Connoisseur: The Japanese
summary: Cherry blossom viewing, or hanami, is a cherished rite in Japan. Some Japanese are also doing it in South Korea.

title: Planning a World Cup Watch Party at a Bar? The ‘FIFA Police’ Are Lurking
summary: Some businesses advertising watch parties in Canada’s two host cities are wary of running afoul of FIFA’s copyright, which protects advertising “World Cup” events.
